en: A: May I ask if Mai-ia is home?
She’s at home, please come in.
A: May I ask if Ibu is home?

ab: Uka aipa, Maaq su na iskilim?
ch: B:她不在，請問你有什麼事?
en: B: She’s not here, what can I do for you?
A: Who is it?
B: Grandpa, I’m Savi, please open the door.
A: Who is it?

ab: Ulih, Mua’ aak, Kilim aak su’u na pisqaitmal.
en: B:Ulih, I’m Mua’, I’ve come to play with you.
A: Long time no see.

ab: Tuza! Minliskin aak su’u. Kava minsuma pababazbaz ata.
ch: B:是啊!我很想念你。快來聊天吧!
en: B:Yeah! I miss you so much. Come on, let’s chat!
A: Please sit down and have some tea.
B: Thank you.
A: Please have some tea.
B:  Your tea is delicious.
A: Let’s eat fruit!

ab: Azuq! Ma ququt-aqun dii a saqalan tu masihal.
ch: B:好喔!這些水果看起來很棒。
en: B: Yes! These fruits look great.
A: How is the fruit?
B:Yes, it’s really delicious.

ch: A:媽媽煮好晚餐了，快來吃吧!
en: A: Mom has cooked dinner, come and eat!
B: Okay, I’ll go wash my hands.

ab: Tamasaza maun, Nii aas tu nata Bunun.
ch: A:盡量吃喝，你又不是外人。
en: A: Try to eat and drink as much as possible, you’re not an outsider.
B: I ate a lot, I’m full.
A: Eat more, don’t be polite.
B:Well, it’s delicious.

ab: Na mudanin aak. Muqnin muqna mapasadu su’u.
ch: A:我要離開了，下次再來看你。
en: A: I’m leaving, I’ll see you next time.
B: Okay, goodbye.

ab: Maqmut in. Na mudanin aak lumaq.
en: A: It’s late, I’m going home.
B:Come again next time!
A: Walk slowly and carefully.

ab: Uu, Haiza ail-uan munhan lumaq nak qaitmal.
en: B: Okay, come to my house sometime.
